name: Mom's Cream Caramels/grandpa's
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a layer of chopped pecans in buttered jelly roll pans and set aside.
2. In a large saucepan, combine sugar, salt, and corn syrup. Cook over medium heat until the mixture reaches the soft ball stage (235°F/113°C on an instant-read thermometer).
3. Gradually add butter and evaporated milk to the sugar mixture, stirring constantly to prevent boiling over.
4. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches the firm ball stage (250°F/121°C on an instant-read thermometer).
5. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract.
6. Pour the caramel sauce into the jelly roll pans, spreading it evenly over the chopped pecans.
7. Allow the caramel to cool thoroughly before cutting into squares with a sharp knife.
8. Wrap each square tightly in wax paper for storage or serving.
